title: Socket Buffer Overflow Detection
dataSources: `sourcetype=custom:socket_stats` from `/proc/net/sockstat` and related files (e.g. drops or backlog fields your script maps to `TCPBacklogDrop`)
app: `Splunk_TA_nix` and a custom scripted input for extended socket stats
spl:
index=os sourcetype=custom:socket_stats host=*
| stats avg(TCPBacklogDrop) as avg_drop by host
| where avg_drop > 0
description: Finds positive rates of listen-backlog drop counters (as represented by your `TCPBacklogDrop` field), which line up with receive drops or service overload on busy listeners.
value: Backlog or socket drops usually come before end-user connection timeouts; fixing buffer sizes, listener scaling, or upstream overload gets cheaper while impact is still partial.
implementation: Extend `sockstat` collection to publish drop counters you care about (field names may differ by kernel; normalize to `TCPBacklogDrop` in the script). Alert on any sustained non-zero `avg_drop` in the window, then filter noisy hosts with baselines in a second iteration.
knownFalsePositives: Kernels that do not publish the field, leaving a literal zero and hiding issuesŌĆövalidate field mapping; microbursts on restart that clear immediately; load tests in lower environments you should suppress by host prefix.

references.title: Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux
Splunk Lantern ŌĆö use case library

detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
ŌĆó You must add fields beyond the stock TA: parse `/proc/net/sockstat`, `/proc/net/sockstat6`, and `netstat`/`ss` as needed, then index as `custom:socket_stats`.

Step 1 ŌĆö Configure data collection
Map kernel metrics into stable names (`TCPBacklogDrop` in this example). If your OS names differ, use `eval` in SPL to alias them.

Step 2 ŌĆö Create the search and alert
The provided SPL alerts on the first sign of non-zero average drops. Add `| where avg_drop > baseline` with a lookup if you need noise control.


Step 3 ŌĆö Validate
Use `ss -lnte` and `nstat` on the host to confirm queuing, and `sar -n TCP,ETCP` (if `sar` is installed) for retrans/queue health during incident windows.

Step 4 ŌĆö Operationalize
Pair with the owning app team: tune `somaxconn`, app accept threads, and load balancer health checks in parallel.
grandmaExplanation: We catch when the system is starting to turn away work at the front door of busy network listeners, so you can fix sizing or load before people see long waits and errors.
